name: Apricot Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the apricots in apple juice for 10 minutes.
2. In a bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, ground cardamom or ginger, and chopped walnuts.
3. In another bowl, beat together vegetable oil, sugar, and egg.
4. Add the soaked apricots with their juice and beaten egg mixture to the dry ingredients.
5. Mix until all ingredients are just blended.
6. Spoon batter into oiled muffin tins or paper muffin cups (sprayed with non-stick spray), filling each cup 3/4 full.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 10 to 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
1 muffin - 182 calories, 1 bread exchange, 1/2 fruit, 2 fat 22 grams carbohydrate, 4 grams protein, 10 grams fat 97 mg sodium, 166 mg potassium, 34 mg cholesterol
Source: Am. Diabetes Assoc. Holiday Cookbook by Betty Wedman, 1986. Shared but not yet tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Nov 93.
Suggestions: Use raisins to test the recipe if you do not have apricots. Leave out walnuts to reduce fat and use 2 egg whites instead of the whole egg to reduce cholesterol if desired.
